content.full_text:
April 4, 1991

HAND DELIVERED

Mr. Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear Scott:

This is a request for written confirmation of the informal advice you provided to me today concerning the Ethics in Government Act of 1990 (Gov't Code §§ 89500 et seq.).

From time to time clients of our firm may sponsor charitable fundraisers involving golf tournaments with meals, refreshments, transportation and entertainment provided in connection therewith. Typically, invitations to participate in the event are extended to persons from the public and private sectors. Public sector attendees include elected state officers and state administrative officials. The sponsor (or sponsors) pay for all costs of the tournament, including greens fees, meals and refreshments, transportation and entertainment. During the event, participants may be asked to make a contribution to a specific charity. The elected state officers and state agency officials in attendance are not asked or expected to make a speech nor are they featured participants in the event.

You advised me that, under these facts, greens fees, meals and refreshments, transportation and entertainment provided to elected state officers and state administrative officials by the sponsor(s) in connection with charity golf tournaments constitute gifts. These gifts are permissible provided that they do not exceed the gift limits imposed by Government Code sections 89504 and 89505. You further advised that none of the payments listed above made by the sponsor(s) in connection with the golf tournament would constitute an honorarium for any of the public officials involved.

Mr. Scott Hallabrin  
April 4, 1991  
Page 2

We understand that your advice, as summarized herein, will remain in effect unless superseded by formal action of the Commission.

We would appreciate confirmation of this advice at your earliest convenience.

Very truly yours,

[signature]
Frederick K. Lowell
